tickers.one_line_thesis: DataSection is betting the Neo Cloud franchise on hyperscaler-linked AI data centres in Japan (Inzai) and Australia, with a FY2026 revenue step-up to ¥33.6B still guided post-Apr-2026 delays; the May 2026 shift to NVIDIA B300 via Compal (~USD 325M) and a disclosed financing bridge improves execution visibility but raises contract-renegotiation, debt-timing, and Giga-order overhang risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
DataSection is in the middle of a high-velocity pivot from legacy AI software/retail-tech services to AI infrastructure ("Neo Cloud") — GPU capacity, proprietary cluster software (TAIZA), and owned/operated AI data centres in Japan, Australia, and abroad. FY2025 consolidated revenue was only ¥2.9B; the revised FY2026 (Mar-26) forecast calls for ¥33.6B revenue and ¥2.8B parent net profit — a step-change driven by hyperscaler-linked data centre contracts and GPU server deployments.

One-line view: Transformational AI-infrastructure compounder-in-the-making with explosive revenue ramp and stra...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Japan's dominant exchange and clearing infrastructure — a Stahl croupier with ~58% operating margins and volume leverage — but earnings are cyclical with market turnover; do not treat FY2025 peak activity as a permanent baseline.

Dominant e-commerce + AWS platform compounding at scale (FY2025 $717B sales, $78B NI) with AWS re-accelerating (+28% Q1-26) and high-margin ads/chips engines emerging — but AI capex has temporarily collapsed FCF, requiring disciplined valuation and capex ROI monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Amazon is a multi-engine compounder: North America retail, International retail, AWS cloud, advertising, devices/chips, and logistics — with AWS and advertising providing the highest-margin growth engines. FY2025 net sales reached $716.9B (+12% YoY) with $77.7B net income; AWS alone generated $128.7B sales and $45.6B operating income (+20% / +15% YoY). Q1 2026 accelerated: net sales $181.5B (+17%), AWS +28% (fastest growth in 15 quarters), operating income $23.9B.

The investment tension is AI capex vs. cash flow: FY2025 capex $131.8B (net) crushed trailing FCF to $11.2B (FY-end) and $1.2B ...

tickers.one_line_thesis: AI data centre developer with accelerating revenue ($126.6M FQ3, +139% YoY) and 600+ MW of signed leases (CoreWeave, hyperscaler). FQ3 GAAP net income $100.9M includes $59.7M discontinued-operation gain (Cloud held-for-sale); continuing ops still loss-making on adjusted basis. Customer concentration and layered debt/preferred stack require capital-structure diligence before conviction sizing.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Applied Digital is a high-power AI data centre developer/operator transitioning from crypto-hosting roots to HPC/AI colocation under long-term leases with CoreWeave and an investment-grade hyperscaler. Fiscal Q3 2026 (ended Feb-28-2026) showed $126.6M revenue (+139% YoY) and $44.1M adjusted EBITDA, but GAAP net loss to common of $100.9M due to Cloud Services held-for-sale accounting, stock-based comp, and buildout costs.

Contracted capacity exceeds 600 MW across Polaris Forge 1 (400 MW CoreWeave), Polaris Forge 2 (200 MW hyperscaler), and Delta Forge 1 (300 MW IT load, ground broken Jan-20...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global alternative-asset compounder earning durable fee streams on $614B fee-bearing capital with scaling insurance/wealth platform ($180B assets), active buybacks, and corporate simplification (BN+BNT merge) — DE-focused valuation, not GAAP NI.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Brookfield Corporation is a global alternative-asset manager and wealth platform earning fees on $614B fee-bearing capital (Q1-26), plus stable cash flows from operating businesses (renewables, infra, real estate, private equity) and a scaling insurance/wealth solutions arm ($180B insurance assets). Q1 2026 distributable earnings (DE) were $1.55B ($0.66/share), with fee-related earnings (FRE) up 11% YoY. Management repurchased >$1B of BN/BAM shares YTD 2026 and is advancing BN + BNT (insurance) combination to simplify corporate structure.

One-line view: High-quality capital allocator compo...

tickers.one_line_thesis: CMSG is a cash-rich OTCQX bitcoin/scrypt miner where marked-to-market digital assets dominate GAAP earnings; the equity is largely a BTC-treasury and hash-share option under Horizon-affiliated governance — watch at 8.7% blended IRR (Marvin floor 7.7%); approved Substacks (LCI seigniorage) add partial treasury credit; fee redesign post-2026 and network-share trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CMSG is an optionality name: loss-making mining ops plus a cash-rich treasury and Horizon-related governance. Normalized owner earnings (~$1.35/sh) blend interest income and breakeven mining, excluding crypto marks. At ~$30 (OTCQX, ~May 26, 2026), base expected annual return at today's price (Lawrence) is 7.7%, below the 15% bar; bull 18.8% requires BTC/treasury re-rating. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is unproven; asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial via NAV floor. We propose watch and size for OTC liquidity; reconcile with any FRMO/Horizon sleeve overlap.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global salvage-vehicle auction toll booth de-rated to ~$33 — oligopoly economics intact; base IRR 9.2% (core override) while monitoring U.S. assignment/fee growth and Q3 FY2026 trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Copart operates a global online salvage-vehicle auction network — a toll-booth on total-loss and remarketed vehicles with extraordinary returns on tangible capital, minimal financial leverage, and a balance sheet that benefits from float (buyer/seller funds held before settlement). FY2025 (ended July 31, 2025) delivered ~$4.6B revenue and ~$1.7B operating income per management narrative; GAAP contract revenue was $3.97B with $1.55B net income (diluted EPS $1.59).

One-line view: Dominant salvage-auction infrastructure with pricing power and network scale; near-term U.S. service-revenue grow...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Wide-moat CRE platform de-rated to ~$33 (−54% YoY); Homes.com/Matterport distort GAAP — watch at 8.6% base IRR until normalized FCF proves out.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
CoStar is the dominant U.S. commercial real estate data and marketplace platform (CoStar Suite, LoopNet, Apartments.com, STR, etc.), expanding into residential (Homes.com) and 3D/digital twin (Matterport, closed Feb 2025). FY2025 revenue was $3.25B (+~8% implied from prior year filings) with 79% gross margin, but GAAP operating income collapsed to $72M and net income to $7M after heavy acquisition amortization, integration costs, and Homes.com marketing investment. Adjusted EBITDA was $480M (~15% of revenue).

One-line view: Wide-moat information monopoly in CRE with expensive residential l...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Decentralized VMS acquirer reinvesting FCFA2S at hurdle rates — base 16.0% IRR supports core stance; GAAP net income secondary to FCFA2S compounding.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Constellation Software is a serial acquirer of vertical market software (VMS) businesses — a decentralized holding company that deploys 100% of free cash flow (FCFA2S) into small acquisitions meeting hurdle rates. FY2025 revenue reached $11.62B (+15% YoY) with FCFA2S of $1.68B (+14%); GAAP net income fell to $586M (−24%) amid earnout accrual volatility and mix. Q1 2026 accelerated: revenue $3.18B (+20%), 6% organic growth (2% adjusted), net income $400M (+131% YoY).

One-line view: Proven capital-allocation machine with recurring-revenue subsidiaries — Pabrai-style compounder if acquisition...

tickers.one_line_thesis: DBS-driven life science/diagnostics platform — hold at 10.1% base IRR (quality override); cyclically tied to biopharma funding and China diagnostics recovery.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Danaher is a life sciences and diagnostics platform company operating through the Danaher Business System (DBS) — lean/Kaizen culture applied to recurring-revenue, razor/blade businesses. Post-Veralto separation (2023), Danaher reports three segments: Biotechnology, Life Sciences, and Diagnostics. FY2025 revenue was $24.6B with operating income $4.69B and net income $3.61B (diluted EPS $5.05). Q1 2026 showed re-acceleration: revenue $5.95B (+~4% vs prior-year quarter implied from segment commentary) and net income $1.03B.

One-line view: High-quality Pabrai/Munger compounder in life science...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Horizon Kinetics–affiliated holding company with concentrated stakes in exchange infrastructure (MIH) and HKHC — a look-through bet on Murray Stahl's ecosystem, not an operating croupier; value = sum-of-parts vs fair-value accounting noise.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
FRMO is not an operating croupier — it is a holding company / intellectual-capital vehicle affiliated with Horizon Kinetics, holding concentrated stakes in market infrastructure (Miami International Holdings), Horizon Kinetics Holding Corp. (HKHC), managed funds, and smaller digital-asset exposures. Fair-value accounting makes reported earnings highly volatile; book value is the more stable anchor.

One-line view: Leveraged bet on Murray Stahl's Horizon ecosystem + exchange stakes (MIH IPO mark-up), with ~82% of equity in a single investment position — attractive only if one trusts the unde...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Search/YouTube ads cash engine plus Google Cloud AI infrastructure (+63% Q1 2026) — wide-moat compounder betting massive capex ($180–190B 2026) on Cloud ROI while navigating AI disruption to Search monetization.

Global exchange and market-infrastructure croupier with 20 consecutive record revenue years and ~80% exchange margins — quality toll booth, but Lawrence base IRR ~11% at $156 sits below the 15% hold band on mid-cycle FCF; watch for pullback or bull-case volume.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Intercontinental Exchange is a canonical Stahl croupier: global exchange, clearing, data, and mortgage-technology infrastructure with minimal principal risk on core operations. FY2025 was the 20th consecutive year of record net revenues ($9.9B, +7% YoY); Q1 2026 accelerated to record net revenues of $3.0B (+20% YoY) with exchange segment margins near 80%.

One-line view: Diversified financial-market toll booth (energy/financial futures, NYSE, fixed income data, mortgage tech) with extraordinary exchange economics and volume leverage — but mortgage-tech GAAP margins remain thin and M&A amort...

human_review.notes: Human override to hold: incumbent croupier sleeve despite base IRR below 15%.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Keweenaw is a micro-cap mineral royalty and land company (~1.1M shares outstanding) holding 1M+ net mineral acres in Michigan/Wisconsin, with primary near-term economics tied to Highland Copper's Copperwood project leases (~$350K/year lease income in FY2024). The 2024 acquisition of 667K gross mineral acres for ~$1.02M (~$1.52/acre) materially expanded optionality.

One-line view: Optionality on Michigan copper/minerals with a patient capital structure — not a dhando compounder today; thesis is asset value + eventual royalty streams, offset by steady-state operating burn and single-tenant c...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global data/index/clearing croupier with 50%+ margins and strong cash conversion; quality franchise at ~£91 implies 12.6% base return — watch below 15% bar.
At ~$633, Meta's Family of Apps funds AI capex and Reality Labs; Lawrence base annual return 5.9% (watch stance) pending proof that $125–145B 2026 capex earns owner cash.

Mesabi Trust is a contractual iron ore pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore (Silver Bay, MN): cash is cyclical commodity + bonus-tier optionality, with ongoing AAA arbitration (commenced Sep 2025) layered on statutory trust simplicity—hold at 8.4% normalized yield for the incumbent royalty sleeve; do not downgrade on depressed Q1 bonus alone.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Mesabi Trust is a passive royalty trust (13,120,010 units outstanding per `MSB/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20251212_rpt20251031_acc0001104659_25_120673.htm`) earning income from pellets and other iron ore products shipped by Northshore Mining from Silver Bay, Minnesota under long-dated leases, with Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. as operator and parent. Cash available to unitholders is almost entirely a function of tons shipped, realized pricing, and whether bonus royalty thresholds are met—not operating execution by the Trust.

tickers.one_line_thesis: U.S. OTC market infrastructure and ATS operator — a Stahl croupier with subscription-heavy fees and expanding disclosure tiers (OTCID) and extended-hours trading — earnings scale with issuer count and activity, not balance-sheet risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
OTC Markets Group operates regulated U.S. OTC equity markets (OTCQX, OTCQB, OTCID, Pink Limited) and SEC-regulated alternative trading systems (OTC Link ATS/ECN/NQB, OTC Overnight, MOON ATS). Revenue is predominantly recurring subscription and market-data fees tied to listed/traded securities — a Stahl croupier model with limited principal risk on core operations.

FY2025 net revenues ~$107.9M (+~6% vs prior year); Q1 2026 net revenues $33.8M (+15% YoY). Diluted EPS $2.58 in 2025 (+14%). Management launched OTCID (July 2025), extended-hours infrastructure, and international "List Local, Tra...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Post-merger diagnostics platform (Quidel + Ortho) with improving adjusted margins but declining revenue — integration and non-respiratory growth must prove out before this is a compounder again.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
QuidelOrtho is a diagnostics company formed by the 2022 merger of Quidel (point-of-care) and Ortho Clinical Diagnostics (lab/transfusion). The combined entity spans immunoassay, molecular, lab instruments, and transfusion — a complex, integration-heavy business, not a Stahl croupier.

FY2025: Reported revenue $2,730.2M (~−2% vs $2,782.9M prior year); adjusted EBITDA $597.0M (margin 21.9%, up from 19.5%). Ongoing restructuring, portfolio simplification, and respiratory revenue normalization post-COVID.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Passive San Juan Basin gas NPI royalty trust — equity yield curve / time arbitrage on temporary Hilcorp capex deficit (no distributions since May 2024); re-rating potential when distributions resume if unit price discounts recoverable NPI rather than terminal wind-down.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
San Juan Basin Royalty Trust is a passive royalty vehicle — not a Stahl croupier and not a Munger moat business. Unit holders receive ~75% net-overriding royalty on gas/oil from Hilcorp-operated San Juan Basin properties (New Mexico). The trust has no employees; Argent Trust Company is trustee.

Critical fact (FY2025): The Trust received no royalty income from Hilcorp in the year ended December 31, 2025 and made no unit-holder distributions. tickers.one_line_thesis: AI Data Cloud platform on consumption pricing: strong product growth and positive FCF, but watch at ~$240 (~6.7% base IRR) until owner cash per share proves at post-earnings run rate.

Essential financial intelligence — ratings, indices, and data subscriptions — a Stahl croupier / index infrastructure compounder with extraordinary margins; earnings leverage to issuance and AUM with cyclicality at the margin.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
S&P Global is a canonical Stahl croupier / index-infrastructure compounder: ratings, indices (AUM-linked fees), Market Intelligence subscriptions, Platts energy benchmarks, and Mobility data — fees on financial and commodity market activity with minimal principal risk.

FY2025: Reported revenue $15.336B (+8% YoY); GAAP diluted EPS $14.66 (+19%); adjusted diluted EPS $17.83 (+14%); adjusted operating margin +140 bps. Returned $6.2B to shareholders (113% of adjusted FCF).

tickers.one_line_thesis: Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Teqnion is a Swedish industrial serial acquirer (founded 2006, listed 2019) owning a decentralized portfolio of niche industrial subsidiaries. Growth via M&A + operational improvement; 2025 included nine acquisitions and restructuring of an underperforming "tail" of portfolio companies.

FY2025: Net sales 1,800 MSEK (+15%); EBITA 203.1 MSEK (+36%, margin 11.3%); organic net sales −5%; EPS 5.74 SEK (+3%); FCF +123% YoY. Board proposed no dividend for 2025.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Permian land, royalty, and water toll at ~$393: durable moat, falsifier-adjusted base -1.0%, NAV overlay ~$211/sh vs price — watch for better entry.

Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong Adj. EBITDA growth ($254M FY2025) and ~2,500 miles of pipe — asset-heavy midstream toll model, not Stahl croupier; thesis blocked by IPO/combination complexity and Five Point sponsor control.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
WaterBridge Infrastructure operates produced-water gathering, disposal, and recycling infrastructure in the Delaware Basin (Permian), primarily under long-term contracts with E&P operators. Listed via IPO September 2025 following combination of legacy water entities — not a Stahl croupier; asset-heavy midstream with customer concentration and sponsor control.

FY2025: Total revenues $525.6M (+66% vs $316.3M 2024); Adjusted EBITDA $254.0M (+68%); operating income $78.9M. ~2,500 miles of water pipelines; significant Five Point (sponsor) voting control and board designation rights.
